rag_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use only valid component names, ports, and data types from the input AADL model
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the original component hierarchy and connections when inserting annex blocks
Ensure all referenced identifiers in AGREE annexes are valid and in scope
Preserve the original AADL property associations and their values
Do not modify the AADL architecture beyond inserting the generated annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Ensure all referenced identifiers in AGREE annexes are valid and in scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not modify the AADL architecture beyond inserting the generated annex blocks
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
